chunks.text: [MassDevice](https://www.massdevice.com/) The Medical Device Business Journal — Medical Device News & Articles | MassDevice # Latest News on MassDevice ## [Edwards gets FDA nod for Triformis Resilia surgical tricuspid valve replacement](https://www.massdevice.com/edwards-fda-triformis-resilia-tricuspid-valve/) June 2, 2026 By [Sean Whooley](https://www.massdevice.com/author/sean-whooley/) Edwards Lifesciences (NYSE:EW) said today that it received FDA approval for Triformis Resilia, its surgical tricuspid valve replacement. Irvine, California-based Edwards says it represents a significant approval for the first surgical tricuspid valve designed specifically for patients with tricuspid valve disease. The company said at the end of 2025 that it earmarked the second half […] Filed Under: [Cardiac Implants](https://www.massdevice.com/category/cardiovascular/structuralheart/cardiac_implants/), [Cardiovascular](https://www.massdevice.com/category/cardiovascular/), [Featured](https://www.massdevice.com/category/featured/), [Food & Drug Administration (FDA)](https://www.massdevice.com/category/regulatory_compliance/food_drug_administration_fda/), [Implants](https://www.massdevice.com/category/implants/), [Pre-Market Approval (PMA)](https://www.massdevice.com/category/regulatory_compliance/food_drug_administration_fda/pre_market_approval/), [Regulatory/Compliance](https://www.massdevice.com/category/regulatory_compliance/), [Replacement Heart Valves](https://www.massdevice.com/category/cardiovascular/structuralheart/replacement_heart_valves/), [Structural Heart](https://www.massdevice.com/category/cardiac_assist_devices/), [Structural Heart](https://www.massdevice.com/category/cardiovascular/structuralheart/) Tagged With: [Edwards Lifesciences](https://www.massdevice.com/tag/edwards_lifesciences/), [FDA](https://www.massdevice.com/tag/fda/), [nitinol](https://www.massdevice.com/tag/nitinol/), [Nitinol Devices & Components](https://www.massdevice.com/tag/nitinol_devices__components/) ## [Pulmera wins FDA clearance for 3D imaging platform](https://www.massdevice.com/pulmera-wins-fda-clearance-for-3d-imaging-platform/) June 2, 2026 By [Sean Whooley](https://www.massdevice.com/author/sean-whooley/) Pulmera announced today that it received FDA 510(k) clearance for its CBeam 3D intraoperative imaging platform. Palo Alto, California-based Pulmera designed CBeam to transform conventional 2D surgical C-arms into intraoperative 3D imaging systems. The technology helps surgical teams visualize complex anatomy during procedures. It enables hospitals to expand access using imaging infrastructure they already own. Elevate Plus brings its advanced technology suite and capabilities to the company’s flagship EPIQ Elite and Affiniti ultrasound systems. Portage, Michigan-based Stryker designed TPX HD to support performance, control and ergonomics in a wide range of challenging orthopedic procedures. These include total joint revisions, minimally invasive surgery and oral maxillofacial procedures. TPX HD incorporates a […] Filed Under: [Featured](https://www.massdevice.com/category/featured/), [Orthopedics](https://www.massdevice.com/category/orthopedics/), [Surgical](https://www.massdevice.com/category/surgical/) Tagged With: [Stryker](https://www.massdevice.com/tag/stryker/) ## [Picard Medical’s SynCardia completes milestone study of next-gen total artificial heart](https://www.massdevice.com/picard-syncardia-milestone-study-emperor-tah/) June 2, 2026 By [Sean Whooley](https://www.massdevice.com/author/sean-whooley/) Picard Medical (NYSE:PMI), parent company of SynCardia, today announced the completion of an in vivo study of its Emperor platform. The company completed an acute in vivo implant study series using the next-generation Emperor Total Artificial Heart (TAH). Its evaluation took place at the University of Arizona and Banner University Medical Center in Tucson, Arizona. Minneapolis-based ShiraTronics completed enrollment in the RELIEV-CM2 pivotal FDA investigational device exemption (IDE) study evaluating the safety and efficacy of the company’s neuromodulation system in adults with chronic migraine. Investigators completed the first implants in the U.S. […] Filed Under: [Clinical Trials](https://www.massdevice.com/category/clinical-trials/), [Implants](https://www.massdevice.com/category/implants/), [Neurological](https://www.massdevice.com/category/neurological/), [Neuromodulation/Neurostimulation](https://www.massdevice.com/category/neuromodulation_neurostimulation/), [Pain Management](https://www.massdevice.com/category/neuromodulation_neurostimulation/pain_management/) Tagged With: [ShiraTronics](https://www.massdevice.com/tag/shiratronics/) ## [Resmed completes $340M acquisition of Noctrix Health](https://www.massdevice.com/resmed-completes-340m-acquisition-noctrix-health/) June 1, 2026 By [Sean Whooley](https://www.massdevice.com/author/sean-whooley/) Resmed (NYSE:RMD) announced today that it completed its previously announced $340 million acquisition of Noctrix Health. San Diego-based Resmed agreed to acquire the medical device company developing clinically validated wearable therapeutics for chronic neurological disorders last month. The acquisition brings a treatment for drug-refractory Restless Legs Syndrome (RLS) to Resmed’s clinical sleep health portfolio. It builds on the existing LEAF platform. Boston-based Apollon and MIT’s Laser Biomedical Research Center (LBRC) published their findings from the peer-reviewed clinical study in the Journal of Diabetes Science and Technology.
